notes: Marked applicable:false for the claudecode adapter — see recipe by_adapter.claudecode.applicable. Triggering claudecode's 5-hour subscription quota in a live recording would burn the maintainer's actual Anthropic subscription; deferred until a mock/env-injection path exists (e.g. an ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL stub that returns a 429 quota-error response). Per the assessment (irrlicht_observes: partial, confidence 0.55), Bucket-A failures (transcript epilogue carrying the error text) land at ready via the normal classifier path; the 'state stays ready under follow-up prompts' bullet from the spec is the partial-observation gap (current parser shows a fast working→ready flicker on follow-up user input). The phases below are the SHAPE future work would assert — they are NOT runnable today and are recorded here as documentation only.

text: FUTURE ASSERTION: session appears in ready
FUTURE ASSERTION: user prompt drives ready → working before the quota error lands
FUTURE ASSERTION: quota-error-bearing assistant line is appended; classifier treats it as turn-end; working → ready. State must not stick in working.
